channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump returning to power after unprecedented comeback, emboldened to reshape American institutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump, who overcame impeachments, criminal indictments and a pair of assassination attempts to win another term in the White House, will be sworn in as the 47th president on Monday, taking charge as Republicans assume unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump promotes new meme coin before taking office on pro-crypto agenda
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has launched a new cryptocurrency token that is soaring in value – and potentially boosting his net worth – just before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigrants in Chicago and other US cities brace for expected Trump deportation arrests
item.description: Immigrants in Chicago and other U.S. cities have been preparing for immigration arrests since President-elect Donald Trump won the November election.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's family circle has a different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago. His youngest son, Barron, was in fifth grade back then.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Civil rights leaders and King family mark MLK Day as a special call to action as Trump takes office
item.description: Events honoring Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy and advocating for his vision of a just, nonviolent society will occur the same day as Donald Trump's second presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vance will make his mark as the first millennial vice president and the potential MAGA torchbearer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As he prepares to become the nation's first millennial vice president, JD Vance is already the presumptive heir to the “Make America Great Again” movement. Vance hasn't been assigned a specific portfolio in the White House like some of his predecessors.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Northern highlights: Alaska's energy, security policies are the guide feds need amid transition, group says
item.description: Future 49, with proponents from civilian pilots to Gov. Mike Dunleavy, is primed to launch to promote Alaskan issues and its energy, economic and national security benefits to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capit
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio met with UAP whistleblower prior to Trump nomination
item.description: Barber said he’s worked in both an official and unofficial capacity for the U.S. government and partners in the intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of people from around the United States rallied in the nation's capital Saturday for women's reproductive rights and other causes they believe are under threat from the incoming Trump administration, reprising the original Women's March days before President-elect Donald
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, ASHRAF KHALIL and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons: Here’s what to know about potential clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to make good on his promises to pardon at least some Jan. 6 rioters on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's family circle has different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mass deportation raids could start in Chicago as soon as Tuesday: Report
item.description: Mass deportation raids threatened in Chicago could start as soon as Tuesday, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic state AGs hurry to preserve Biden gun control
item.description: Trump, in a previous campaign speech, said he would "roll back every Biden attack on the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: After major 2024 defeats, the Democratic Party searches for a new direction
item.description: The Democratic Party begins 2025 with several looming questions. Among them: who will lead its national party apparatus, and how it will handle President-elect Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Nicole Mitchell trial delayed until after legislative session
item.description: A judge has granted DFL Senator Nicole Mitchell’s request to delay her trial until after the Minnesota legislative session ends.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court sides with GOP, cancels special election key to House control
item.description: In late December, DFL Gov. Tim Walz set a special election for Jan. 28. But the Supreme Court sided with Republicans who said Walz acted too quickly in setting the date and postponed the election indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Blinken tells AP he's worried Trump administration may abandon key Biden foreign policy initiatives
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Outgoing Secretary of State Antony Blinken told The Associated Press that he hopes the incoming Trump administration will press forward with key points in President Joe Biden’s foreign policy, including on the Middle East and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury will work to prevent hitting debt ceiling: Yellen
item.description: The department has in the past deployed what are known as “extraordinary measures,” or accounting maneuvers, to keep the government operating.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor says his meeting with Trump covered jobs and ceasefire deal, but not his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams said his meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday included discussions of bringing manufacturing jobs back to the city and the Gaza ceasefire deal — but not the mayor’s federal bribery case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that he was commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses, using his final days in office on a flurry of clemency actions meant to nullify prison terms he deemed too harsh.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court upholds law banning TikTok if it’s not sold by its Chinese parent company
item.description: The Supreme Court has unanimously upheld the federal law banning TikTok beginning Sunday unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump, New York City Mayor Eric Adams to meet at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams is meeting President-elect Donald Trump on Friday at Mar-a-Lago, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ellison joins 13 states to defend health care coverage for DACA recipients
item.description: The effort is in response to a legal challenge led by Kansas and several other states against a Biden administration rule.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Giuliani reaches tentative settlement with Georgia election workers
item.description: Attorneys for Rudy Giuliani and two former Georgia election workers said Thursday they have reached a tentative settlement, a remarkable twist that would end the workers’ extensive litigation to collect their $146 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay native Pam Bondi goes through Senate confirmation hearings to become U.S. Attorney General
item.description: Pam Bondi grew up in Temple Terrace, she worked in the Hillsborough County State Attorney's office as a prosecutor, and she served two terms as the elected Florida State Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: “Kids can get online porn through gaming systems, tablets, phones, computers. Let me just say that content filtering for all those different devices, I can say from personal experience, is difficult to keep up with,” said Justice Amy Coney Barrett, who has seven children.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Group behind failed Florida recreational marijuana amendment trying again for 2026
item.description: The group behind the 2024 ballot measure that would have enshrined recreational marijuana access in the Florida Constitution is giving it another shot in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: County Board of Supervisors votes to hold special election to fill District 1 seat
item.description: In a swift and unanimous vote, the County Board of Supervisors has agreed to a special election to fill the vacancy left behind by Nora Vargas.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5
